zh.lifeSummary: Arnulf de Montgomery於1066年出生在法國諾門迪。他至少有1個女兒。他去世於1122年在他的家鄉享年56歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Arnulf de Montgomery于1066年出生在法国諾門迪。他至少有1个女儿。他去世于1122年在他的家乡享年56岁。

nameMeanings.name: Arnulf
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

nameMeanings.name: Montgomery
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Transferred use of the surname, originally a Norman baronial name from various places in Calvados. The place name is derived from Old French mont ‘hill’ + the Germanic personal name Gomeric ‘power of man’. It has never been common as a given name, although it was given additional currency by the American actor Montgomery Clift ( 1920–66 ), and during and after the Second World War by the British field marshal, Bernard Montgomery ( 1887–1976 ).

en.lifeSummary: Arnulf de Montgomery was born in 1066, in Normandy, France. He had at least 1 daughter. He died in 1122, in his hometown, at the age of 56.

fr.lifeSummary: Arnulf de Montgomery est né en 1066, à Normandie, France. Il a eu au moins 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1122, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 56 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Arnulf de Montgomery wurde 1066 in Normandie, Frankreich geboren. Er hatte mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb 1122 im Alter von 56 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Arnulf de Montgomery è nato il nel 1066 nel Normandie, Francia. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlia. Morì nel 1122, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 56 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Arnulf de Montgomery(은)는 1066년 프랑스 노먼디에서 태어났다. 그는 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1122년 그의 고향에서 56세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Arnulf de Montgomery nasceu em 1066, em Normandie, França. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 1122, em sua cidade natal, com 56 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Arnulf de Montgomery родился в 1066 г. в местности норманди, Франция. У него была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Он умер в 1122 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 56 лет.
